title: OpenDesk — Concurrency Ceilings
description: Per-layer saturation for the voice stack, per docs/VOICE-SCALING.md: a voice agent is a stack of independent layers, each with its own concurrency ceiling — real capacity is the LOWEST ceiling, and this board exists to watch the bottleneck move between layers. Rows: media/worker plane, inference plane (LLM/STT/TTS), application plane (Postgres pools, gateway), pipeline (Kafka lag). Panels referencing the `voice_*` family go live with the voice-agent-runtime /metrics instrumentation (same Wave as the AI & Voice dashboard); Postgres/Kafka panels render gracefully empty until postgres_exporter / kafka_exporter series are scraped — see each panel's description.

panels.title: Worker plane — gate at 0.7, HPA up at 0.5
Active voice sessions (concurrent calls)
panels.description: voice_active_sessions gauge from voice-agent-runtime /metrics. THE unit of load: one session holds 1 SFU room + 1 worker job + 1 STT stream + LLM rate share + 1 TTS stream (+ optional SIP channel). Compare against the capacity-planning worksheet ceilings (docs/runbooks/capacity-planning.md).

panels.title: Worker CPU vs admission gate (0.7) and HPA trigger (0.5)
panels.description: Per-pod CPU as a fraction of request (2 cpu). Horizontal guides: 0.5 = HPA scale-up trigger, 0.7 = LOAD_THRESHOLD where workers refuse new jobs. Healthy scaling: pods hover below 0.7 because the HPA adds capacity at 0.5. Sustained > 0.7 = worker plane is the ceiling; scale maxReplicas or add compute-optimized nodes (never burstable).

panels.title: Inference plane — usually the real ceiling
LLM latency p50/p95 by model
panels.description: voice_llm_call_duration_seconds (qwen3:8b via Ollama by default; MiniMax-M2 when the API adapter is active). Rising p95 at flat call rate = Ollama parallel slots (OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL) saturated — GPU capacity, not CPU, is the binding constraint.

panels.title: LLM token rate (throughput ceiling proxy)
panels.description: voice_llm_tokens_total rate by model. LLMs cap on RATE, not concurrency: watch tokens/s plateau while voice_llm p95 climbs — that plateau is the Ollama/GPU (or provider TPM) ceiling. Self-hosted: add GPU or raise OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL. API mode: the provider's TPM cap.

panels.title: STT / TTS stream latency (p95)
panels.description: voice_stt_duration_seconds (faster-whisper) and voice_tts_duration_seconds (piper). STT/TTS cap on CONCURRENT STREAMS: whisper streams per GPU/CPU core and piper worker count are the ceilings. p95 creeping toward the turn budget (~1s STT, ~0.5s TTS first-byte) with rising active sessions = inference plane is next to fail.

panels.title: Ollama saturation (queue/parallel proxy)
panels.description: In-flight LLM calls as a proxy for Ollama parallel-slot occupancy (OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL). Sustained plateau at the configured slot count = queue forming upstream; callers hear it as slower first-token. Until a native ollama queue metric is scraped this derives in-flight calls from the voice_llm histogram.

panels.title: Postgres connections per database (pool pressure)
panels.description: pg_stat_activity_count from postgres_exporter, grouped by datname — the closest available series to per-service pool usage (pgxpool exposes no Prometheus metrics today; pool MaxConns is set via PG_MAX_CONNS, see docs/runbooks/capacity-planning.md §DB pools). Voice calls fan out per-turn queries (booking availability, identity context): connections approaching the pool/pg max_connections injects DEAD AIR while every other dashboard looks green. Panel renders empty until postgres_exporter is scraped — absence of data here is a monitoring gap, not proof of headroom.

panels.title: APISIX gateway rate & bandwidth (voice routes)
panels.description: apisix_http_status rate and apisix_bandwidth for /voice/* routes. The gateway is rarely the ceiling, but webhook/SIP-ingress spikes show here first; bandwidth matters because voice signalling + TTS clips traverse it.

panels.title: Kafka consumer lag by group
panels.description: kafka_consumergroup_lag from kafka_exporter, per consumer group (transcripts, booking commands, notification fan-out). Lag is not a call-path ceiling, but transcript/CRM/event consumers falling behind during a call ramp is an early symptom of downstream saturation. Renders empty until kafka_exporter series are scraped.

panels.title: Media plane — LiveKit node CPU & participants
panels.description: SFU is CPU/bandwidth-bound: node CPU for livekit=true nodes plus, when scraped, livekit_participant_total / livekit_room_total from the SFU's :6789 Prometheus port. Rollout reminder: old+new capacity during the 5h drain window.
